marketState: neutral-bullish — extreme near-term weather demand offset by storage surplus and absent seasonality data
summary: Seasonality data is entirely missing (vsMedian, probHigher, typicalMove, reliability all null), removing the highest-priority input and forcing lower conviction across both trade horizons. Weather is the dominant live signal: 1-7D demand runs 29.5% above the 5-year average and above the 5-year maximum range, while 8-16D demand is even more extreme at +41.3% above max — a historically rare double-reading that is near-term bullish for price. Price at $3.28 sits slightly above the current fair-value midpoint of $2.96 but remains inside the $2.46–$3.46 band; importantly, the fair-value model itself rises to $3.15 over 21 days, implying convergence upward rather than downward reversion pressure. Managed money is net short at just the 17th five-year percentile, providing structural short-squeeze fuel if the weather catalyst holds. Storage at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average plus a bearish +6 Bcf injection surprise cap sustained upside, keeping the upper fair-value band near $3.45–$3.56 as a realistic ceiling for any weather-driven pop.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality data — the primary long-horizon input — is completely absent, making historical base-rate analysis impossible. Storage surplus of +151 Bcf vs 5y with above-consensus injections creates a structural bearish weight that makes a sustained 3-12 week rally unlikely without a major supply shock. The weekly trend is bearish (EMA 50<200, weekly trend flagged bearish), and while COT short positioning at the 17th percentile is contrarian bullish, that signal is more relevant to a short-squeeze over days-to-weeks than to a sustained multi-month trend change.
longTerm.trigger: Seasonality data populates AND storage injections miss consensus (below forecast) for at least 2 consecutive weeks — confirming weather demand is drawing down the surplus; OR managed money net short percentile drops to the 5th percentile or lower before a price reversal candle, maximizing short-squeeze potential.
